mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for at least 2 months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% sustained, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over 3 consecutive months signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, signaling economic strength that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS-HOUST signal falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive signals fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 102.0 index level sustained for 2 months indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, supporting corporate profitability and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts toward growth optimism.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO > 102.0 sustained, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously declines, this joint deterioration signals accelerating labor market weakness that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 315.0 index level while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 5.0%, real rates are negative or near-zero, creating a monetary policy lag that eventually forces rate hikes and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets anticipate tightening.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,300 thousand units for 2 consecutive months signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as construction and related sectors face headwinds.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with real GDP growth (GDPC1) both expanding at above-trend rates indicates broad-based supply-side strength and productivity gains that support equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days in expansion regimes.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
